# Commit Message Template

Use this template for clear, conventional commits.

---

# <type>(<scope>): <subject>
# |      |         |
# |      |         +-> Imperative mood, lowercase, no period
# |      |
# |      +-> Scope of the change (optional but recommended)
# |
# +-> Type: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, style, perf, chore

# Types:
#   feat      - A new feature
#   fix       - A bug fix
#   refactor  - Code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
#   docs      - Changes to documentation
#   test      - Adding or updating tests
#   style     - Changes that don't affect code meaning (formatting, etc)
#   perf      - Code change that improves performance
#   chore     - Build, dependency, config, tooling changes

# Examples:
#   feat(auth): add JWT token refresh mechanism
#   fix(posts): handle null title validation
#   refactor(db): extract query builder to utility
#   docs(api): update endpoint documentation
#   test(auth): add comprehensive token refresh tests

# Body (optional, wrap at 72 chars):
# Explain WHAT and WHY, not HOW. Include:
# - Motivation for the change
# - Contrast with previous behavior
# - Reference related issues/PRs

# Footer (optional):
# - Fixes #123
# - Closes #456
# - Related-To #789

---

# SUBJECT EXAMPLES:

# Good:
#   ✅ feat(auth): add JWT token refresh
#   ✅ fix(post): prevent null titles
#   ✅ refactor(db): simplify user queries

# Bad:
#   ❌ Added token refresh feature
#   ❌ fixed bug in posts
#   ❌ refactored stuff
